Foundation Pipeline Fixture

Coherence

The framework claims that coherence requires a stable reference across domains. This claim means that local order cannot explain itself without a reference. Does this question get extracted?

The master equation states that every major coherence factor contributes to the final output. In this section we prepare the reader for the next idea.

\chi = G \cdot M \cdot E \cdot S_eff \cdot T \cdot K \cdot R \cdot Q \cdot F \cdot C

Claims

A broken system cannot restore the reference state using only error-preserving operations. The closure theorem predicts that internal operations remain trapped inside the error class. Therefore, this sentence is a transition and should not be kept.

External grace functions as the required outside operator in the theological register. The analogy maps to phase-transition language when the broken phase cannot restore symmetry from within.

Boundaries

The paper does not claim that physics proves Christianity by itself. It only claims that the same formal constraint appears across domains. The model should be downgraded if self-generated operations reach the reference class.

The channel equation states that signal-to-noise ratio changes the available capacity. The reader should reject the strong version if the claimed bridge is only metaphorical.

$C_i = A_i \log_2(1 + T_i/D_i)$

(Smith, 2024) provides one citation and John 3:16 provides one scripture anchor.
